Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash To Stay And Or For A Protective Order Re California Department Of Transportation Business Records Subpoena To Forensic Laboratory Division Office Of The Chief Medical Examiner And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JAIME JUAREZ'S MOTION TO QUASH, TO STAY AND OR FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER RE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION BUSINESS RECORDS SUBPOENA TO FORENSIC LABORATORY DIVISION OFFICE OF THE CHIEF MEDICAL EXAMINER AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS.

Appearances: Lora Dorin Curtis of the California Department of Transportation (510.433.9100) appearing for defendant Department of Transportation; Adam Hackett of Ford Walker Haggerty & Behar (562.983.2582) appearing via Court Call for defendant Jaime Juarez.

Defendant Department of Transportation stipulates to the motion being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. Notice by Mr. Hackett to contest the matter was not timely. Defendant Jaime Juarez is deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem deciding the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The Motion to Quash, To Stay and/or for a Protective Order filed by Defendant Jaime Juarez is DENIED. The subpoena issued by Defendant State of California, Department of Transportation to the Custodian of Records: Forensic Laboratory Division - Office of the Chief Med. Examiner seeks information relevant to the issues in this case, it is not overbroad and the information sought is not privileged. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Margaret Niver, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
